workflow.critical: The workflow execution engine is governed by: {project-root}/_bmad/core/tasks/workflow.xml
You MUST have already loaded and processed: {installed_path}/workflow.yaml
Communicate all responses in {communication_language} and generate all documents in {document_output_language}
TWO-PHASE ITERATIVE CODE REVIEW with model-awareness and lifecycle document integration.
Phase 1: Test Suite Quality Review — adversarial verification of tests against ACs, edge cases, best practices.
Phase 2: Full BMAD Code Review — complete adversarial review of implementation, architecture, security.
Fix ALL issues, then REPEAT both phases until all substantive issues are resolved.
Do not review files outside the application source. Always exclude _bmad/, _bmad-output/, .cursor/, .windsurf/, .claude/ directories.

step.goal: Load story, check model awareness, discover changes
step.action: Use provided {{story_path}} or ask user which story file to review
Read COMPLETE story file
Set {{story_key}} = extracted key from filename
Set {{epic_num}} = first number from story key
Parse sections: Story, Acceptance Criteria, Tasks/Subtasks, QA Test Specification, Dev Notes, Dev Agent Record
step.critical: Fresh-eyes (cross-model) review is the default practice — same-model review has diminishing returns.
step.action: Read "Dev Agent Record → Agent Model Used" from the story file
Set {{dev_model}} = the model name/version recorded
Determine your own model identity (the model currently executing this review)
Set {{reviewer_model}} = your model identity

check.output:
**MODEL-AWARENESS WARNING**
The dev agent used **{{dev_model}}** to implement this story. You are running as **{{reviewer_model}}** — this is the SAME model family.
Cross-model review catches architectural and type-design issues that same-model self-review misses. Story 1.2 experience: same-model R1-R2 caught implementation bugs but missed crash-on-error-emit and type-safety issues. Cross-model R3 found both immediately.
**Options:** 1. Proceed anyway (I understand diminishing returns of same-model review) 2. Switch to a different model for this review (recommended) 3. Cancel and restart with a different model
check.ask: How would you like to proceed?

check.action: HALT — user will restart with different model

step.action: Check if git repository detected

check.action: Run `git status --porcelain` to find uncommitted changes
Run `git diff --name-only` to see modified files
Run `git diff --cached --name-only` to see staged files
Compile comprehensive file list from git + story File List

step.action: Compare story's Dev Agent Record File List with actual git changes
Note discrepancies: - Files in git but not in story File List - Files in story File List but no git changes
Load {project_context} for coding standards (if exists)
Set {{review_file}} = {implementation_artifacts}/{{story_key}}-review.md

check.action: Load existing review document
Log: "No lifecycle review document found — will create one during review"

step.action: Set {{round}} = 1
Set {{review_log}} = "" (accumulates round-by-round findings)

step.critical: ADVERSARIAL TEST REVIEW — Find every test gap, every untested path, every lazy shortcut!
This phase is anchored to the QA Test Specification (if present) and Acceptance Criteria.
step.output: **--- ROUND {{round}}, Phase 1: Test Suite Quality Review ---**
step.action: Check if story has a "## QA Test Specification" section

check.action: Use it as the review anchor — every item in the spec must be covered by actual tests
Generate test expectations from ACs directly (this is a gap — note it as a finding)

step.action: Identify all test files from story File List and git changes
Read EVERY test file completely

For EACH Acceptance Criterion: 1. Identify which test(s) cover this AC 2. Verify the test actually asserts the AC's requirements (not just a placeholder) 3. If NO test covers this AC → HIGH finding: "AC {{n}} has no test coverage" 4. If test exists but assertions are weak/incomplete → MEDIUM finding
For EACH edge case in QA Test Specification (or derived from ACs): 1. Search test files for coverage 2. Missing edge case test → MEDIUM finding with specific test description needed
step.critical: When one case in a family of patterns is tested, ALL siblings must be tested.
step.action: Identify test families (e.g., testing invalid values for one message type): 1. Find all tests that test ONE case in a pattern family 2. Identify all siblings that should have equivalent tests 3. Missing sibling test → HIGH finding: "Sibling coverage gap: [tested case] has test but [sibling] does not"
step.critical: Any method that registers EventEmitter listeners MUST have a test verifying listenerCount returns to baseline after completion.
step.action: Identify all methods in the implementation that call .on(), .once(), .addListener(), or .addEventListener(): 1. For each: verify a test checks listenerCount before and after 2. Missing cleanup test → HIGH finding: "[method] registers listeners but has no listenerCount verification"
step.critical: Every mock must be tested with ALL its possible return values.
step.action: Identify all mocks/stubs in test files: 1. For each mock: determine all possible return values it could produce 2. Check that each return value appears in at least one test 3. Untested mock return value → MEDIUM finding: "Mock [name] returns [value] but no test exercises this path"

Check for test anti-patterns: - Tests that pass without assertions (empty test bodies) - Tests that only check truthy/falsy instead of specific values - Tests that mock the thing they're testing - Duplicate test descriptions (copy-paste errors) - Missing afterEach cleanup - Tests dependent on execution order
Compile Phase 1 findings with severity: HIGH / MEDIUM / LOW
Set {{phase1_findings}} = compiled findings

step.critical: ADVERSARIAL CODE REVIEW — Validate every claim, challenge every assumption!
step.action: Review git vs story File List discrepancies: 1. Files changed but not in story File List → MEDIUM (incomplete documentation) 2. Story lists files but no git changes → HIGH (false claims) 3. Uncommitted changes not documented → MEDIUM (transparency)

For EACH Acceptance Criterion: 1. Read the AC requirement 2. Search implementation files for evidence 3. Determine: IMPLEMENTED, PARTIAL, or MISSING 4. If MISSING/PARTIAL → HIGH finding with specific gap description
For EACH task marked [x]: 1. Read the task description 2. Search files for evidence it was actually done 3. If marked [x] but NOT DONE → CRITICAL finding 4. Record specific proof (file:line)

For EACH implementation file (excluding test files): 1. **Security**: injection risks, missing validation, auth issues 2. **Performance**: inefficient loops, missing caching, N+1 patterns 3. **Error Handling**: missing try/catch, poor error messages, unhandled promise rejections 4. **Architecture**: violations of project patterns (socketFactory DI, EXTERNAL_CONTEXT, etc.) 5. **Code Quality**: complex functions, magic numbers, poor naming, dead code 6. **TypeScript**: type safety, proper use of strict mode, no `any` escape hatches
Verify all protocol/API requirements from architecture are implemented: - Command formats correct - Response parsing handles all documented values - Connection lifecycle followed
Compile Phase 2 findings with severity: CRITICAL / HIGH / MEDIUM / LOW
Set {{phase2_findings}} = compiled findings

step.goal: Present findings and fix all issues
step.action: Merge {{phase1_findings}} and {{phase2_findings}}
Categorize: CRITICAL (must fix), HIGH (must fix), MEDIUM (should fix), LOW (nice to fix)
Count: {{critical_count}}, {{high_count}}, {{medium_count}}, {{low_count}}

check.ask:
I found {{critical_count + high_count + medium_count}} substantive issues. Options:
1. **Fix all automatically** — I'll fix all CRITICAL, HIGH, and MEDIUM issues now 2. **Show details first** — deep dive into specific findings before fixing 3. **Fix selectively** — choose which issues to address
Choose [1], [2], [3], or specify issue numbers:

check.if: user chooses 1 or selects issues to fix
check.action: Fix all selected CRITICAL, HIGH, and MEDIUM issues in the code
Add/update tests as needed
Update File List in story if files changed
Run full test suite to verify fixes don't break anything
Log fixes applied to {{review_log}}: "Fixed: [description] in [file:line]"

check.output: Fresh adversarial pass found no new substantive issues in Round {{round}} — review is CLEAN.
check.action: GOTO step 6

step.goal: Iterate — fresh adversarial pass until clean
step.critical: Each subsequent round is a FRESH ADVERSARIAL PASS — not just verification of previous fixes.

Approach the code as if you have never reviewed it before. Look for NEW issues, not just fix confirmation. The goal of each round is to find everything wrong, not to confirm that nothing is wrong. A round is CLEAN only when a genuinely fresh adversarial review finds zero substantive issues.
step.action: Increment {{round}} by 1

check.output: **WARNING:** Review has reached Round {{round}}. This is unusual. Consider whether remaining issues are truly substantive or are diminishing-returns nitpicks.
check.ask: Continue reviewing (y) or finalize with current state (n)?

check.action: GOTO step 6

step.output: **Starting Round {{round}} — fresh adversarial pass on all code (not just verifying previous fixes)...**
step.action: GOTO step 2

step.goal: Write findings to lifecycle review document and add lessons learned

check.action: Load {{review_file}}
Create {{review_file}} with header: # Story {{story_key}} — Lifecycle Review Created: {{date}} ## Story Spec Review *Not run*

step.critical: Lessons Learned must include actionable process optimization recommendations and test efficiency assessment — not just a list of what was found.
step.action:
Analyze the review process and generate Lessons Learned:
**Process Optimization Recommendations:** - What could the story spec have included to prevent these review findings? - Were there patterns in the findings that suggest a systematic gap? - Should any new items be added to the QA Test Specification template? - Were any findings the same type as previous stories' findings (recurring issues)?
**Test Efficiency Assessment:** - Were tests well-structured and easy to review? - Were there redundant tests that could be consolidated? - Were there test gaps that a better QA Test Specification would have caught? - Ratio of Phase 1 (test) findings vs Phase 2 (code) findings — what does this indicate?
**Recommendations for Future Stories:** - Specific items to add to story creation process - Patterns to watch for in similar stories - Memory items to save for project-level learning
Save {{review_file}}

step.goal: Update story status and sync sprint tracking

check.if: all CRITICAL and HIGH issues fixed AND all ACs implemented
check.action: Set {{new_status}} = "done"
Update story Status field to "done"

check.if: CRITICAL or HIGH issues remain OR ACs not fully implemented
check.action: Set {{new_status}} = "in-progress"
Update story Status field to "in-progress"

step.action: Save story file

check.action: Load the FULL file: {sprint_status}
Find development_status key matching {{story_key}}
Update development_status[{{story_key}}] = "done"
Save file, preserving ALL comments and structure
Update development_status[{{story_key}}] = "in-progress"
Save file, preserving ALL comments and structure
